title: Give it an input
prompt: Functions can take input, called a parameter. Here `name` is handed to you. Return the text: Hello, <name>! — for example greet('Tyler') returns 'Hello, Tyler!'. Join pieces of text with the + sign.

details.restate: You're given a name. Hand back one single string: the word Hello, a comma and space, then the name, then an exclamation mark.

io.because: the name is dropped into the middle of the greeting
whatever name comes in goes into the same pattern

terms.meaning: an input the function receives; here `name` holds whatever text the caller passed in
